channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Domain wall motion-driven magnetic convolutional accelerator
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 14 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-68041-4

The authors demonstrate a magnetic compute-in-memory platform that performs convolution using domain wall motion, enabling highly energy- and area-efficient processing for future AI hardware.
item.dc:title: Domain wall motion-driven magnetic convolutional accelerator
item.dc:creator: Bingqian Dai
Tianyi Wang
Albert Lee
Shijie Xu
Chin-Chung Chen
Kin Wong
Dingyi Li
Malcolm Jackson
Yang Cheng
Puyang Huang
Yaochen Li
Chao Yun
Qingyuan Shu
Haoran He
Lixuan Tai
Hanshen Huang
Tien-Kan Chung
Yanglong Hou
Kang L. Wang

item.title: Decoding the gradient-distributed colour centers in electrochromic WO<sub>3</sub>
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 14 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-68046-z

Multislice electron ptychography reveals that gradient-distributed ions induce progressive lattice distortions from bulk to surface, driving monoclinic γ-WO3 electrochromic materials to transform into tetragonal and cubic phases and forming the gradient-distributed colour centers.
item.dc:title: Decoding the gradient-distributed colour centers in electrochromic WO<sub>3</sub>
item.dc:creator: Sikang Xue
Jizhe Cui
Chuchu Zhou
Min Shen
